findings.evidence: `rg "isJHolomorphic(WithinAt|On).*transport|transport_iff"` finds only `isJHolomorphicAt_transport_iff` at line 109 and `isJHolomorphic_transport_iff` at line 119; the parallel product API has within/on iff lemmas at `JHolomorphicProd.lean:153` and `:167`.
findings.fix: Add `isJHolomorphicWithinAt_transport_iff` and `isJHolomorphicOn_transport_iff`, with the same image-set convention as the forward lemmas.
findings.issue: `IsJHolomorphicWithinAt.transport` and `IsJHolomorphicOn.transport` expose forward subset/setwise transport, but there are no corresponding `*_transport_iff` lemmas, so users cannot rewrite transported within/on goals back without manually applying inverse transport.

findings.evidence: `JHolomorphicTransport.lean:109` and `:119` define the iff lemmas without attributes, while the analogous product iff lemmas are all `@[simp]` at `JHolomorphicProd.lean:140`, `:153`, `:166`, and `:180`.
findings.fix: Mark the pointwise and global transport iff lemmas `@[simp]`, and do the same for the missing within/on iff lemmas once added.
findings.issue: The transport iff lemmas are characteristic normal-form lemmas but lack `@[simp]`, so `simp` will not reduce transported J-holomorphicity to the original predicate.

summary: The transport API exposes all four J-holomorphic predicates but only characterizes two of them, and the characteristic iff lemmas are not registered for simplification.
